commit.message:
feat(audit): add X-Subject-Id and X-Subject-Type fallback headers (#168)

* feat(audit): add X-Subject-Id and X-Subject-Type fallback headers

X-Blaxel-Subject-Id and X-Blaxel-Subject-Type remain priority;
X-Subject-Id and X-Subject-Type are used as fallbacks when the
Blaxel headers are absent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix formatter for msg

* The null-byte placeholder was always escaped by logrus's needsQuoting check,
causing both bytes.Replace calls to silently fail and leaving placeholder text
in every log line. Replace with a post-format regex that finds unquoted msg=
values and wraps them with strconv.Quote.

click outside itself and close immediately.

- apple-web-app 메타 태그 추가

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add domain discovery, self-assessment, and roadmap quality rules

- Add Domain Discovery step (Step 1b) to both Quick Start and Custom Build paths.
  When the user mentions an external system/database/API, the LLM must investigate
  it BEFORE proposing features — read the README, schema, available data.
- Add mandatory Self-Assessment after post-build verification. LLM must critically
  evaluate: does the data make sense (both sides of conversations)? Would the user
  keep this open all day? Is anything obviously missing? Is the roadmap useful?
- Add roadmap quality rules: prioritize by user impact, describe concrete benefits
  not technical tasks, base features on actual available data, no generic filler.

Replace local-cors-proxy with custom Node.js proxy server

only Node.js built-in http/https modules - no npm install needed.

concepts with Android/JUnit parallels.

and makes dayLabel internal for test access.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(ui): built-in font system with font() API (#1079) (#1103)

* feat(ui): add built-in font system with font() API (#1079)

Introduce font() descriptor function and compileFonts() for first-class
font support in the theme system. Fonts are declared once and the framework
generates @font-face CSS, --font-* custom properties, and <link rel="preload">
tags automatically.

- font() creates branded FontDescriptor with weight, style, display, src, fallback
- compileFonts() generates @font-face blocks, CSS vars, and preload tags
- Theme integration: defineTheme({ fonts: { sans } }) merges font vars into :root
- SSR integration: preload tags injected into <head> before CSS
- Production SSR handler: inlineCSS support with XSS-safe escaping
- Shared sanitize.ts: sanitizeCssValue() and escapeHtmlAttr() utilities
- woff2-only validation: throws for non-woff2 src paths
- Landing page migrated from manual 46-line boilerplate to font() API
- Full test coverage: 23 font tests, 25 theme tests, SSR integration tests
- Type-level tests: font.test-d.ts validates all type constraints
- Subpath exports updated for font and compileFonts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ui): harden font/sanitize security and narrow types (#1103)

- sanitizeCssValue: strip <> and single quotes to prevent style tag breakout
- escapeHtmlAttr: escape single quotes (')
- FontDescriptor: narrow style to 'normal' | 'italic', display to union type
- compileFonts: validate font keys match /^[a-zA-Z0-9-]+$/
- Add comprehensive sanitize.test.ts
- Add </style> breakout and key validation tests to font.test.ts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
brain-server: add global error handlers and fix silent async catches

Prevent silent crashes from unhandled promise rejections by adding
process.on('uncaughtException') and process.on('unhandledRejection')
handlers to the entry point. Also add .catch() with logging to all
fire-and-forget async IIFEs across the codebase, and replace silent
catch blocks with logged ones.

commit.message:
Split prototype.html into public/ static site structure

public/
├── index.html   — HTML shell
├── styles.css   — all styles
└── js/
    ├── config.js  — DEBUG, SLIDE_CONFIG, ICON_CONFIG
    ├── parse.js   — CSV/TSV parsing and Squarespace/legacy matching
    ├── images.js  — image fetch, WebP→JPEG conversion, line estimation
    ├── pptx.js    — slide rendering and PPTX generation
    └── app.js     — app state, import flow, review table

No logic changes — pure structural split. prototype.html retained for reference.

feat: archive closed deals from pipeline to Closed tab (#172)

- Add "✅ Archive" button on closed deals in pipeline sections
- Archived deals hide from pipeline but remain visible in Closed tab
- Uses pipeline_archived status to differentiate from full archive
- Works for both seller and buyer side pipelines
- GCI summary cards still include all closed deals

Co-authored-by: Derik J Bannister <derikbannister9@Deriks-MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix abstention threshold, embedding resilience, and large doc stability (#25, #26, #27)

#25: Lower default abstention threshold from 0.300 to 0.001 to match
RRF score range (~0.001-0.01 with k=60). Add configurable
COVALENCE_SEARCH_ABSTENTION_THRESHOLD env var, wire to SearchService.

#27: Make embedding storage resilient — log warnings instead of
aborting ingestion on individual embedding failures (chunks, sources,
nodes). Skip re-embedding nodes that already have embeddings from
prior ingestion runs.

#26: Batch extraction processing to limit memory pressure on large
documents. Process extractable chunks in batches of 2×concurrency
instead of join_all over all futures. Individual extraction failures
are logged and skipped rather than aborting the full pipeline.

commit.message:
Refactor edge profiles to nullable and add lamination method UI (#388)

* fix: replace hardcoded pencil_round/arris edge slugs with null defaults

Bug 1 fix (FIX-40MM): quote-setup-defaults.ts and room-presets.ts
hardcoded 'pencil_round' and 'arris' slug strings as default edges.
These slug strings were written directly to the DB, causing phantom
polishing charges (Bug 3) because the calculator treated any non-null
edge value as "finished".

All piece type defaults now return null edges (raw). The tenant-
configurable default_edge_type_id from pricing_settings (QF-1) is
the correct source for edge defaults when set.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01VPoqhVtPs6MR6VeNo5bSNU

* fix: remove thickness>=40 auto-LAMINATED fallback and Mitred→PencilRound enforcement

Bug 2 fix: pricing-calculator-v2.ts forced LAMINATED on any piece with
thickness_mm >= 40, even when lamination_method was NONE. Lamination
method is now purely an explicit user choice.

Bug 6 fix: MiniPieceEditor.tsx used thickness === 40 as isMitred check,
forcing Pencil Round on edge clicks for all 40mm pieces. Now checks
lamination_method === 'MITRED'. Removed pencilRoundId useMemo and the
old Mitred→PencilRound enforcement block entirely — the mitre IS the
finished edge per March 5 correction.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01VPoqhVtPs6MR6VeNo5bSNU

* fix: add lamination method selector for 40mm pieces, fix interface types

Bug 5 fix: Added 3-button selector (None/Laminated/Mitred) shown when
thickness > 20mm, replacing the text-only "Laminated" indicator. Selector
appears in all 3 shape sections (RECTANGLE, L_SHAPE, U_SHAPE).

Added lamination_method to InlinePieceData interface and handleSave
payload. Replaced ugly cast+wrong fallback that inferred LAMINATED
from thickness with proper local state from piece.lamination_method.

Also added lamination_method to MiniPieceEditor WizardPiece interface
to fix TS error from Fix 2.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01VPoqhVtPs6MR6VeNo5bSNU

---------

commit.message:
feat: add external newsletter broadcast API

Endpoints for newsletter-pc to send broadcasts via PainClinics.com:
- POST /api/broadcast (create + send/schedule)
- POST /api/broadcast/test (test send to single address)
- GET /api/broadcast/:id (status + analytics)
- DELETE /api/broadcast/:id (cancel queued)
- GET /api/broadcasts (list history)
- GET /api/lists (subscriber lists + counts)

Auth via X-API-Key header, subscriber lists map to contacts tags,
Mailgun delivery with CAN-SPAM unsubscribe footer injection.

commit.message:
Improve typing accuracy: weighted signals, fairness fixes, stress arrows

- Drop calibration phase: all 25 questions are now LLM-generated adaptive
- Remove behavioral signals from engine, prompts, handler, and frontend
- Add weighted multi-type signals: each option signals 3-5 types with weights
- Add per-type signal coverage tracking to detect neglected types
- Router enforcement: force broad cuts for neglected types after Q8
- Pair question cap (max 3) with inconsistency detection
- Broad cut weight cap to prevent early snowball
- Extend floor protection through Q15
- Add stress arrows and core avoidance to probe guide for all 9 types
- Clean up dashboard: remove behavioral signals and contradictions
- Add simulation script for testing with simulated 6w5

Add inline entry price editing to RR details page

- New PATCH /api/rr-watchlist/{uuid}/entry-prices endpoint accepts
  put_option_quote, call_option_quote (and short_call_option_quote for
  Collar) and persists them to the database
- Net entry_price is recalculated: call_quote * call_qty - put_quote * put_qty
  (Collar also subtracts short_call_quote * short_call_qty)
- RR details page gains "Edit Prices" button on the Option Legs card;
  clicking it turns entry price cells into number inputs
- On Save: PATCH API is called, then all dependent values update client-side
  without a page reload: per-leg Change %, Net Entry Price, and the summary
  Change $ / Change % row
- Cancel restores original values; Collar short call leg also editable
- 7 new unit tests covering service and API endpoint; all 379 tests pass

Test Metrics:
- New tests: 7, Updated: 0, Removed: 0
- Ran: 379 tests in ~141s

commit.message:
feat: add Text.split, lines, join, bytes, to_int

New string operations:
- split(s, delimiter): split string into list of substrings
- lines(s): split by newline, equivalent to split(s, "\n")
- join(parts, sep): join list of strings with separator
- bytes(s): convert string to list of byte values (Int)
- to_int(s): parse decimal integer, returns Option Int
- to_float(s): stub for future float parsing

Also add `use List` import and mark all exported functions `pub fn`.
Helper functions (split_acc, join_acc, bytes_acc, parse_digits) are
private to the module.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: kea
repository.description: A statically-typed language with algebraic effects.  The compiler tracks what your functions do — not just what they return.

commit.message:
feat: enforce pub visibility for cross-module access and fix intramodule calls

- Extend pub enforcement from cross-package-only to all cross-module access.
  Private `fn` declarations are now inaccessible from other module files,
  not just other packages. Struct namespaces (not file modules) are exempt
  from the check so struct const fields remain accessible within the same
  compilation unit.
- Add qualify_intramodule_calls() to rewrite bare function references in
  module function bodies to qualified names during module merge, preventing
  name collisions when modules share function names (e.g. Text.is_empty vs
  List.is_empty). This fixes SIGSEGV when calling Text.to_int and Text.split.
- Make generated test functions (__kea_test_case_N) exempt from visibility
  checks so the test runner can call them cross-module.
- Mark lifted impl block methods as public: true. Trait methods are always
  part of a public contract regardless of whether the `pub` keyword appears
  in the method body.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: kea

commit.message:
fix: use stdin piping + join output for claude --print in auto-bugfix.ps1

Root cause analysis:
- Positional args and splatting both fail: PowerShell mangles multi-line
  strings when passing as native command arguments
- Stdin piping WORKS: claude receives the full prompt via pipe
- JSON extraction failed because PowerShell captures command output as
  an array of lines, not a single string

Fixes:
1. All 5 claude calls use pipe syntax: $prompt | claude --print ...
2. Scan output joined into single string: (...) -join "`n"
3. JSON regex changed to greedy match: \[\s*\{[\s\S]*\}\s*\]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: server-side Google OAuth initiation to fix state_mismatch

Cross-origin fetch (vercel.app → railway.app) causes state_mismatch
because the OAuth state cookie set in the cross-origin response is
not properly sent back when Google redirects to the API callback.

Solution: add GET /auth/google on the API that:
1. Makes an internal POST to Better Auth sign-in/social
2. Forwards the Set-Cookie (OAuth state) in a same-site response
3. Redirects the browser to Google

Since the browser navigates directly to railway.app (not via fetch
from vercel.app), the state cookie is set in a same-site context
and is correctly included when Google redirects to /api/auth/callback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix system fields (display_precision) leaking into view mode

section-view.php had an inline field filter that only checked for tabs,
button_groups, and a regex of known system suffixes — but missed
_display_precision and _vis0 patterns. Replaced with a single call to
SectionRegistry::is_system_field() which is the authoritative system
field detector and covers all SKIP_TYPES, SKIP_KEY_PATTERNS, and
SKIP_NAMES in one place.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite deposit detection to use real tx hashes, add custom wager input

- Replace balance-based deposit polling with transaction-based tracking:
  ETH native uses block scanning with real tx hashes, SOL uses
  getSignaturesForAddress with per-wallet cursor tracking, SPL USDC
  uses parsed transaction token balance diffs
- Add custom wager amount input to WagerSelector (1 to balance, integers)
- Add server-side wager validation (finite, 0-10000, rounded to 2dp)
- Add 19 DepositMonitor unit tests and 10 WithdrawalProcessor unit tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: program_dates — weekly schedule layer with date-range search

New table: program_dates (program_id, start_date, end_date,
cost_from, cost_to, before_care, after_care)
  - Unique constraint on (program_id, start_date, end_date)
  - 359 rows across 92 programs populated from session_date dump

Pipeline:
  - db/migrate_program_dates.py: one-time + re-runnable migration from dump
  - db/sync_from_dump.py: Section 6 refreshes program_dates on every sync
  - core/cssl.py: enrich_with_dates() attaches slots to all results;
    date_from/date_to params JOIN program_dates for week-specific search
  - core/intent_parser.py: IntentResult gains date_from, date_to fields
  - core/session_manager.py: date_from/date_to merged as scalars
  - intent_parser_system_prompt.md: date extraction rules + example
  - ui/results_card.py: weekly slot pills + before/after care badges
